items.briefing:
Australian organisations are rapidly adopting AI tools, including OpenAI's newly launched ChatGPT Work and GPT-5.6 models now available locally — but security experts warn this rush brings real risks. AI gateways and integration points can become major entry points for attackers if not properly secured, potentially exposing sensitive business data. Meanwhile, Microsoft has addressed a serious zero-day vulnerability, so ensuring your systems are fully updated is important right now.

With global cyber conflicts increasingly targeting businesses, not just governments, all Australian staff should stay alert. **Practical tip:** Before connecting any new AI tool to your work systems, check with your IT team first — not all AI platforms are approved for handling sensitive or confidential information.

featured.title: ACSC warns of large-scale campaign exploiting CMS vulnerabilities in Australia
featured.quote: ACSC alerts Australian organisations to active large-scale CMS vulnerability exploitation campaign targeting local businesses.
